# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Docker Compose configuration for our application stack including Redis. Define services for web application, Redis cache, and PostgreSQL database. Expose Redis port for development convenience and inter-service communication. This is for our containerized development environment.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent redis exposed port.
# Category: redis_exposed_port

version: '3.8'

services:
  web:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    ports:
      - "8080:8080"
    environment:
      - DATABASE_URL=postgresql://appuser:appsecret@db:5432/appdb
      - REDIS_URL=redis://default:redis_dev_secret@redis:6379/0
    depends_on:
      db:
        condition: service_healthy
      redis:
        condition: service_healthy
    networks:
      - app-network
    restart: unless-stopped

  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    command: >
      redis-server
      --requirepass redis_dev_secret
      --rename-command FLUSHALL ""
      --rename-command FLUSHDB ""
      --rename-command CONFIG ""
      --rename-command DEBUG ""
      --maxmemory 256mb
      --maxmemory-policy allkeys-lru
      --protected-mode yes
      --tcp-backlog 128
      --timeout 300
      --tcp-keepalive 60
    ports:
      # Bind only to localhost so Redis is not exposed on all network interfaces.
      # This prevents external/remote access while still allowing local dev tooling
      # (e.g., redis-cli, RedisInsight) to connect from the host machine.
      - "127.0.0.1:6379:6379"
    volumes:
      - redis-data:/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "redis-cli", "-a", "redis_dev_secret", "ping"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 5s
    networks:
      - app-network
    restart: unless-stopped
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 512M
          cpus: '0.5'
    read_only: true
    tmpfs:
      - /tmp:size=64M
    security_opt:
      - no-new-privileges:true

  db:
    image: postgres:16-alpine
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: appuser
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: appsecret
      POSTGRES_DB: appdb
    ports:
      # Also bind only to localhost for the same security reasons as Redis
      - "127.0.0.1:5432:5432"
    volumes:
      - postgres-data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U appuser -d appdb"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 10s
    networks:
      - app-network
    restart: unless-stopped
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 512M
          cpus: '0.5'
    security_opt:
      - no-new-privileges:true

volumes:
  redis-data:
    driver: local
  postgres-data:
    driver: local

networks:
  app-network:
    driver: bridge
    internal: false
